title: trigonella infesta
intro: Trigonella infesta, also known as Trigonella foenum-graecum when not infested, is a plant species native to the Middle East and South Asia, belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The infested version is a weed, while the non-infested version is a crop plant with medicinal properties, valued for its seeds that contain a compound called trigonelline. The seeds of Trigonella infesta have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, diabetes, and inflammation, due to their antiseptic, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often used as a powder or oil to treat various health issues, and can also be consumed as a tea or added to food for its medicinal benefits.
